initial_program:
% Count columns (N) and number of nonzero cells (K)
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
n_nonzero(K) :- K = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% Grid size S = N * K
size(S) :- S = N * K.

% Count diagonal cells for each nonzero input cell
count_diag(I,V) :- #count{R,C : diag(R,C,I,V)}.

% Enforce that each diagonal has exactly S - I cells
:- count_diag(I,V) = size(S) - I.

% Diagonal cells for each input nonzero cell
diag(R,C,I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0,
                  R + C = size(S) - 1 + I,
                  C >= I,
                  R >= 0,
                  R < size(S),
                  C < size(S).

% Predicate for being on a diagonal (exists any I,V)
is_diag(R,C) :- diag(R,C,I,V).

% Output rows and columns
output_row(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_col(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Diagonal cells get their value
output(R,C,Colour) :- is_diag(R,C), input(0,I,V), V != 0, Colour = V, output_cell(R,C).

% Nondiagonal cells are zero
output(R,C,Colour) :- not is_diag(R,C), output_cell(R,C), Colour = 0.

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Count columns (N) and number of nonzero cells (K)
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
n_nonzero(K) :- K = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% Grid size S = N * K
size(S) :- S = N * K.

% Count diagonal cells for each nonzero input cell
% Enforce that each diagonal has exactly S - I cells
:- #count{R,C : diag(R,C,I,V)} = size(S) - I.

% Diagonal cells for each input nonzero cell
diag(R,C,I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0,
                  R + C = size(S) - 1 + I,
                  C >= I,
                  R >= 0,
                  R < size(S),
                  C < size(S).

% Predicate for being on a diagonal (exists any I,V)
is_diag(R,C) :- diag(R,C,I,V).

% Output rows and columns
output_row(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_col(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Diagonal cells get their value
output(R,C,Colour) :- is_diag(R,C), input(0,I,V), V != 0, Colour = V, output_cell(R,C).

% Nondiagonal cells are zero
output(R,C,Colour) :- not is_diag(R,C), output_cell(R,C), Colour = 0.

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Count columns (N) and number of nonzero cells (K)
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
n_nonzero(K) :- K = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% Grid size S = N * K
size(S) :- S = N * K.

% Count diagonal cells for each nonzero input cell
% Count diagonal cells for each nonzero input cell
count_diag(I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0, I >= 0, I < size(S), #count{R,C : diag(R,C,I,V)}.
% Enforce that each diagonal has exactly S - I cells
:- count_diag(I,V) = size(N,K,S) - I.
diag(R,C,I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0,
                  R + C = size(S) - 1 + I,
                  C >= I,
                  R >= 0,
                  R < size(S),
                  C < size(S).

% Predicate for being on a diagonal (exists any I,V)
is_diag(R,C) :- diag(R,C,I,V).

% Output rows and columns
output_row(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_col(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Diagonal cells get their value
output(R,C,Colour) :- is_diag(R,C), input(0,I,V), V != 0, Colour = V, output_cell(R,C).

% Nondiagonal cells are zero
output(R,C,Colour) :- not is_diag(R,C), output_cell(R,C), Colour = 0.

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
n_nonzero(K) :- K = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V), V != 0}.

% Grid size S = N * K
size(S) :- S = N * K.

% Count diagonal cells for each nonzero input cell
% Count diagonal cells for each nonzero input cell
count_diag(I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0, I >= 0, I < size(S), #count{R,C : diag(R,C,I,V)}.
% Enforce that each diagonal has exactly S - I cells
:- count_diag(I,V) = size(N,K,S) - I.
diag(R,C,I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0,
                  R + C = size(S) - 1 + I,
                  C >= I,
                  R >= 0,
                  R < size(S),
                  C < size(S).

% Predicate for being on a diagonal (exists any I,V)
is_diag(R,C) :- diag(R,C,I,V).

% Output rows and columns
output_row(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_col(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Diagonal cells get their value
output(R,C,Colour) :- is_diag(R,C), input(0,I,V), V != 0, Colour = V, output_cell(R,C).

% Nondiagonal cells are zero
output(R,C,Colour) :- not is_diag(R,C), output_cell(R,C), Colour = 0.

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

count_diag(I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0, I >= 0, I < size(S), #count{R,C : diag(R,C,I,V)}.
% Enforce that each diagonal has exactly S - I cells
:- count_diag(I,V) = size(N,K,S) - I.
diag(R,C,I,V) :- input(0,I,V), V != 0,
                  R + C = size(S) - 1 + I,
                  C >= I,
                  R >= 0,
                  R < size(S),
                  C < size(S).

% Predicate for being on a diagonal (exists any I,V)
is_diag(R,C) :- diag(R,C,I,V).

% Output rows and columns
output_row(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_col(0..size(S)-1) :- size(S).
output_cell(R,C) :- output_row(R), output_col(C).

% Diagonal cells get their value
output(R,C,Colour) :- is_diag(R,C), input(0,I,V), V != 0, Colour = V, output_cell(R,C).

% Nondiagonal cells are zero
output(R,C,Colour) :- not is_diag(R,C), output_cell(R,C), Colour = 0.

% Exactly one colour per output cell
1 { output(R,C,Colour) : colour(Colour) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.
